scene_description:
**Foreground to Background Description:**  

**Left foreground:**  
- A person wearing a light gray poncho and dark cap stands on a low, weathered concrete wall.  
- Adjacent to the person, a white building has a large rectangular mural (no text) with a brown border, depicting a landscape of mountains and clouds. Below the mural, a small rectangular window with dark brown shutters is visible. A door with dark brown panels is also present on this building.  
- A wooden balcony with dark brown railings is attached to the upper floor of the same white building. The building’s facade has a smooth upper section and a textured lower section.  

**Left midground:**  
- A sidewalk runs parallel to the street, where a person in a checkered shirt walks toward the background.  
- The white building continues, featuring a red fabric awning over a doorway (no text or symbols).  

**Right foreground:**  
- A black motorcycle with red and silver details is parked on the right side of the street. The motorcycle has no visible license plate.  
- Next to the motorcycle, a white building has a red brick lower section. The building features a dark brown door with a small rectangular panel and a window with a metal grid above the door (the window’s shutter is open).  
- Two wooden utility poles (one leaning) are positioned on the right side of the street. The poles have multiple black wires, insulators, and a horizontal street lamp head mounted on one pole. The poles also support additional electrical equipment (e.g., transformers).  

**Center midground:**  
- The narrow street is paved with cracked concrete. A circular manhole cover is centered in the road. The street slopes upward.  
- A white vehicle (no visible license plate) is on the road, further up the street.  

**Center background:**  
- The street continues upward, flanked by white buildings with red brick lower sections. One building in the distance has a red fabric awning over a doorway.  
- More buildings with similar architectural features (e.g., wooden balconies, dark brown doors) are visible.  

**Background:**  
- The street leads to a hill covered in dense green trees (no specific tree type identifiable).  
- Overhead, multiple black wires crisscross between utility poles.  
- The sky is overcast and light gray.  

**Key Details (per Category):**  
- **TEXT & SIGNS:** No visible text on signs, murals, or buildings.  
- **INFRASTRUCTURE:** Two wooden utility poles (with wires, insulators, transformers) and a street lamp head (on a pole) are present. No bollards, traffic lights, fire hydrants, or guardrails.  
- **VEHICLES & PLATES:** A black motorcycle (no plate) and a white vehicle (no plate) are visible. The motorcycle is on the right side of the road.  
- **ROAD SURFACE & MARKINGS:** Concrete road with cracks, a central manhole cover, and no lane markings. The road slopes upward with a low concrete wall (curb) on the left.  
- **BUILDINGS & ARCHITECTURE:** White facades with red brick lower sections, dark brown doors/windows, wooden balconies, and one building with a red awning.  
- **VEGETATION & TERRAIN:** A hilly background with dense green foliage. The street slopes upward, indicating a hilly terrain.

details.name: wooden utility poles
details.reason: The presence of transformers and insulators on wooden poles is common in certain tropical or developing regions.
details.focused_description: Three wooden utility poles are visible. The central pole is tall, cylindrical, and vertical, constructed from weathered wood with a grayish-brown hue, showing signs of aging and exposure. It features metal brackets, electrical insulators, and a transformer mounted on it. The pole to the left is shorter, cylindrical, and vertical, made of weathered grayish-brown wood, with a streetlight and a small rectangular utility box attached. The leaning pole on the right is cylindrical, weathered (darker brown), and tilted at an angle, with metal brackets securing wires and cables.

details.name: street lamp head
details.reason: The specific style of the street lamp attached to the pole can be a regional indicator.
details.focused_description: The street lamp head is a dark-colored, cylindrical light fixture attached to the end of a horizontal arm extending from the pole. It has a rounded shape with a smooth surface, likely made of metal or composite material. No visible text or markings are present on the lamp head. The object appears intact and functional.

details.name: wooden balcony
details.reason: This architectural feature is often found in colonial or tropical-style buildings.
details.focused_description: The wooden balcony features a rectangular structure with vertical wooden spindles (balusters) that are turned. The wood is brown, showing weathered and worn condition with visible damage and peeling in some areas. The base of the balcony also exhibits exposed and deteriorated wood.

details.name: red brick wall
details.reason: The combination of white facades with red brick lower sections is a specific architectural trait.
details.focused_description: A red brick wall is visible, forming a horizontal lower section of the building's exterior. The wall consists of uniformly arranged red bricks, with a consistent rectangular shape running parallel to the street. The material is brick, and the wall appears intact with no visible damage or markings. The color is a solid red.

details.name: driving side
details.reason: The motorcycle is positioned on the right side of the street, indicating right-hand traffic.
